headlines.title: Watch: Residents flee as cars and houses burn in Belfast
headlines.description: Police and firefighters help families leave their homes amid disorder following a knife attack.

headlines.title: Illegal mini-marts to shut for up to 12 months under law change prompted by BBC
headlines.description: Under current rules, shops breaking the law can only be closed for up to six months in England and Wales.

headlines.title: US strikes Iran in response to downing of military helicopter
headlines.description: President Donald Trump earlier accused Iran of shooting down the US helicopter over the Strait of Hormuz and vowed to respond.

headlines.title: BBC cancels planned Doctor Who Christmas special
headlines.description: The BBC said the decision was made "after careful consideration" and had "not been taken lightly".

headlines.title: Who will win the World Cup? BBC pundits make their predictions
headlines.description: Who will win, who might spring a surprise and how will England and Scotland do? BBC pundits predict what will happen at the 2026 World Cup.

headlines.title: Model who alleges Kanye West choked her tells BBC she felt 'suffocated and scared'
headlines.description: West claims the encounter on a music video set was part of a "provocative theatrical performance".

headlines.title: Advanced radiotherapy for prostate cancer to cut sessions from 20 to five
headlines.description: Some men in England with the disease will now be offered an advanced form of treatment on the NHS.

headlines.title: The UK could hit 28C this weekend - is another heatwave on the way?
headlines.description: Temperatures are expected to rise by the end of the week where we could see another heatwave developing, Simon King explains

headlines.title: How to watch the key World Cup matches - and keep your job
headlines.description: Football fans and bosses share their strategies to balance late night kick offs with work the next day.

headlines.title: With no team in World Cup, China fans rally around a red card-happy referee
headlines.description: Ma Ning, known for his stern style, has sparked viral memes and attracted major brand sponsorships.

headlines.title: Cymru connections: Who can Wales fans back?
headlines.description: It's the return of a conundrum that Welsh football fans had to deal with for 64 years before 2022: Who to support at the World Cup?

headlines.title: Twenty new players to look out for
headlines.description: BBC Sport TV and radio commentators pick the players they think could be the breakout stars.

headlines.title: 'People didn't believe I was sleeping rough': The UK's hidden homeless women
headlines.description: Two thirds of the women counted in a charity-led census would not have been recorded using the government method.

headlines.title: Three questions Bill Gates could face as he testifies to Congress on Epstein
headlines.description: The Microsoft co-founder has repeatedly denied any wrongdoing or knowledge of his former associate's illegal activity.

headlines.title: 'My son and daughter play for England, I still pinch myself'
headlines.description: Nigel James says "I still pinch myself" that two of his children represent England's football teams.

headlines.title: Watch: BBC goes into jungle with Myanmar's civil war rebels
headlines.description: The BBC’s Quentin Sommerville travels to Myanmar - without the permission of the authorities - to meet a group of rebel fighters.

headlines.title: My wife's 'unadoptable' baby was left to die at unmarried mothers' home
headlines.description: A new report condemning an unmarried mothers' home is welcomed by survivors and relatives.

headlines.title: Hundreds of twitchers flock to see African bird in UK for first time
headlines.description: It is the first time the African bird has been recorded in the UK.

headlines.title: World Cup referee denied US entry arrives home to hero's welcome in Somalia
headlines.description: Omar Artan vows to officiate at the 2030 World Cup instead after arriving home in Somalia in the wake of being banned from entering the United States.

headlines.title: Inside Myanmar, rebels are losing ground as military forces men into army
headlines.description: The BBC travels with rebels to frontline positions in Myanmar to see how the war is unfolding.

headlines.title: Manhunt under way in South Africa after 12 killed in mass shooting in Johannesburg
headlines.description: Another nine people were injured at an informal settlement in Cleveland late on Tuesday.

headlines.title: Texas teen sentenced to 35 years for killing fellow student at athletics event
headlines.description: The fatal incident during a high school athletics event in a Dallas suburb drew national attention.

headlines.title: Pakistan launches deadly air strikes in Afghanistan, reigniting tensions
headlines.description: The fresh strikes come after weeks of relative calm in the restive border region between the two countries.

headlines.title: Boy, 8, helps save grandad after capsized kayak drifts two miles off coast
headlines.description: An eight-year-old boy and his grandad drifted more than two miles off the Vale of Glamorgan coast.

headlines.title: BBC News app
headlines.description: Top stories, breaking news, live reporting, and follow news topics that match your interests

headlines.title: What We Know About The Belfast Knife Attack So Far
headlines.description: Police and political leaders in Northern Ireland have called for calm.

headlines.title: Americanswers… on 5 Live! Why did Trump walk out of his TV interview with Meet The Press?
headlines.description: And why is JD Vance posting about Henry Nowak?

headlines.title: Who will win the World Cup? BBC pundits make their predictions
headlines.description: Who will win, who might spring a surprise and how will England and Scotland do? BBC pundits predict what will happen at the 2026 World Cup.

headlines.title: Keane and Fernandes clear air after row over 'lie'
headlines.description: Roy Keane clears the air with Manchester United captain Bruno Fernandes after he misquoted the Portuguese - which led to  Fernandes accusing him of lying.

headlines.title: Real Madrid's £130m Alvarez bid laughed off by Atletico
headlines.description: Real Madrid make an offer of 150m euros (£130m) for striker Julian Alvarez, but city rivals Atletico Madrid turn it down.

headlines.title: The wild hip-hop parties that started Tuchel's journey to England boss
headlines.description: Thomas Tuchel is bidding to become the first foreign manager to win the World Cup. BBC Sport charts the start of his journey to becoming England boss.
